Ellen Gunne Tomes
(from 11 January 1571 to 10 January 1572 – 3 August 1601)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Marston Sicca, Gloucester is now Long Marston, Warwickshire.

John Tomes and Ellen Gunne were married about 1586.

Their children born in Long Marston, Warwckshire (Gloucestershre):

zh.lifeSummary: 當Ellen Gunne於1571年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾時，她的父親Richard Long Gunne III31歲，母親Ann Fullwood30歲。她於1584年8月20日與John Tomes結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1601年8月3日在英國格盧斯特斯爾朗 馬爾斯頓享年30歲，埋葬在英國瓦維其可爾馬爾斯頓 格林。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ellen Gunne于1571年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾时，她的父亲Richard Long Gunne III31岁，母亲Ann Fullwood30岁。她于1584年8月20日与John Tomes结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1601年8月3日在英国格盧斯特斯爾朗 馬爾斯頓享年30岁，埋葬在英国瓦維其可爾馬爾斯頓 格林。

nameMeanings.name: Gunne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Mechelle, Pierre.
Variant of   Gano  , a surname of French origin.

variants.name: Gano
meanings: Altered form of French Gaineau, itself a variant of Ganeau: from the ancient Germanic personal name Gagino, from gagin ‘gain, win’. The surname Gaineau is very rare in France. Compare Ganoe and Gunnoe .

History: The Huguenot Étienne Gaineau or Gayneau from La Rochelle in Charente-Maritime, France, married Lydia Mestereau in La Rochelle in 1653, died in Staten Island, NY, in 1706. His name is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (as Étienne Gaineau or Ganeau or Geneau or Gano) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America (as Etienne Gaineau or Gano).

nameMeanings.name: Ellen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Helen , although now no longer associated with that name. Initial H- tended to be added and dropped rather capriciously, leading to many doublets ( compare for example Esther and Hester ). It is also sometimes used as a short form of Eleanor , to which, however, it is unrelated.

variants.name: Eleanor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Ellen Gunne was born in 1571, in Gloucestershire, England, her father, Richard Long Gunne III, was 31 and her mother, Ann Fullwood, was 30. She married John Tomes on 20 August 1584. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 3 August 1601, in Long Marston, Gloucestershire, England, at the age of 30, and was buried in Marston Green, Warwickshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Ellen Gunne est née en 1571, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Long Gunne III, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Ann Fullwood, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé John Tomes le 20 août 1584. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 3 août 1601, à Long Marston, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 30 ans, et a été enterrée à Marston Green, Warwickshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Ellen Gunneが1571年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアで生まれた時，父Richard Long Gunne IIIは31歳，母Ann Fullwoodは30歳であった。1584年8月20日にJohn Tomesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1601年8月3日にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアロング マーストンで30歳で死去し，イギリスワーウィクシュアイアマーストン グレンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Ellen Gunne(이)가 1571년 영국 그루스스터실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Long Gunne III(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ann Fullwood(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1584년 8월 20일 John Tomes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1601년 8월 3일 영국 그루스스터실르 론그 마스턴에서 30세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 워윅실르 마스턴 그린에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ellen Gunne родилась в 1571 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Long Gunne III было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Ann Fullwood, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Tomes 20 августа 1584 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 3 августа 1601 г. в местности лонг марстон, глоусестиршир, Англия в возрасте 30 лет и была похоронена в местности марстон грин, варвиккшир, Англия.
